name: Business Management or Entrepreneurship
description: An applied course covering the core principles of business management and entrepreneurship, from leadership and organizational design to startup strategy and pitching.

modules.title: Functions of Management (POLC)
modules.objectives: Define the four functions of management: Planning, Organizing, Leading, and Controlling Explain how each function contributes to organizational success Apply the POLC framework to a real-world business scenario
modules.readingNote: OpenStax Principles of Management — Chapter 1: Introduction to Management.
modules.videoNote: The four functions of management: Planning, Organizing, Leading, Controlling explained.
modules.video2Note: Management fundamentals: what managers actually do in organizations.
modules.practiceNote: Complete the review questions from OpenStax Principles of Management Chapter 1.
modules.assignment: Choose a real business (your school, a local restaurant, or a company you know) and apply the POLC framework to analyze how it operates. For each function (Planning, Organizing, Leading, Controlling), describe one specific example of how the business applies that function. Then identify one area where you think management could improve.

modules.title: Leadership Styles & Theories
modules.objectives: Distinguish between transactional and transformational leadership Compare autocratic, democratic, and laissez-faire leadership styles Apply situational leadership theory to different workplace scenarios
modules.readingNote: OpenStax Principles of Management — Chapter 13: Leadership.
modules.videoNote: Leadership styles explained: autocratic, democratic, and laissez-faire.
modules.video2Note: Transformational vs. transactional leadership: what's the difference?
modules.practiceNote: Complete the review questions from OpenStax Principles of Management Chapter 13.
modules.assignment: Describe a leader you admire (can be historical, business, or personal). Identify which leadership style(s) they primarily demonstrate and which theory best explains their approach. Then describe a situation where their leadership style might be less effective and suggest an alternative approach.

modules.title: Organizational Structure & Design
modules.objectives: Compare flat and tall organizational structures Explain the concepts of span of control, chain of command, and departmentalization Distinguish between functional, divisional, and matrix organizational structures
modules.readingNote: OpenStax Principles of Management — Chapter 10: Organizational Structure and Change.
modules.videoNote: Organizational structures explained: functional, divisional, and matrix.
modules.video2Note: Span of control and chain of command in organizational design.
modules.practiceNote: Complete the review questions from OpenStax Principles of Management Chapter 10.
modules.assignment: Draw an organizational chart for a small business with at least 12 employees across three departments. Label the chain of command, span of control for each manager, and type of departmentalization used. Then explain why you chose this structure and what its advantages and disadvantages are.

modules.title: Human Resources & Motivation
modules.objectives: Explain the recruitment, selection, and onboarding process in organizations Apply Maslow's Hierarchy of Needs and Herzberg's Two-Factor Theory to employee motivation Describe key HR practices: performance appraisal, training, and compensation
modules.readingNote: OpenStax Principles of Management — Chapter 14: Motivating Employees.
modules.videoNote: Maslow's Hierarchy of Needs applied to the workplace and employee motivation.
modules.video2Note: Herzberg's Two-Factor Theory: hygiene factors vs. motivators at work.
modules.practiceNote: Complete the review questions from OpenStax Principles of Management Chapter 14.
modules.assignment: You manage a team of five customer service representatives. Two of them are underperforming and seem disengaged. Using at least two motivation theories (e.g., Maslow, Herzberg, or McClelland), diagnose potential causes of their disengagement and propose three specific, actionable HR interventions to improve motivation and performance.

modules.title: Operations & Supply Chain
modules.objectives: Define operations management and its role in delivering value Explain the key stages of a supply chain from raw materials to end customer Describe concepts including inventory management, quality control, and lean production
modules.readingNote: OpenStax Principles of Management — Chapter 11: Managing Your Business Operations.
modules.videoNote: Supply chain management explained: from raw materials to the customer.
modules.video2Note: Lean manufacturing and just-in-time production principles.
modules.practiceNote: Complete the review questions from OpenStax Principles of Management Chapter 11.
modules.assignment: Map the supply chain of a product you use every day (smartphone, sneakers, coffee, etc.). Identify at least five stages in the supply chain, name a specific company or actor at each stage, and identify one vulnerability or risk at each stage. Then propose one improvement to make the supply chain more efficient or resilient.

modules.title: Business Planning Process
modules.objectives: Explain the purpose and key sections of a business plan Conduct a basic SWOT analysis for a business idea Describe how market research informs a business plan
modules.readingNote: OpenStax Entrepreneurship — Chapter 11: Entrepreneurial Finance and Accounting.
modules.videoNote: How to write a business plan: key sections and what investors look for.
modules.video2Note: SWOT analysis explained with a real business example.
modules.practiceNote: Complete the review questions from OpenStax Entrepreneurship Chapter 11.
modules.assignment: Choose a business idea (real or hypothetical). Conduct a SWOT analysis (at least 3 items in each quadrant). Then write the executive summary section of a business plan (300–400 words), covering: the problem you solve, your target customer, your solution/product, and your revenue model.

modules.title: Entrepreneurship Mindset & Opportunity
modules.objectives: Define entrepreneurship and the characteristics of successful entrepreneurs Apply opportunity recognition frameworks to identify market gaps Distinguish between invention and innovation in entrepreneurship
modules.readingNote: OpenStax Entrepreneurship — Chapter 1: The Entrepreneurial Perspective.
modules.videoNote: What is entrepreneurship? The mindset, traits, and opportunities.
modules.video2Note: How to identify a business opportunity: frameworks and real examples.
modules.practiceNote: Complete the review questions from OpenStax Entrepreneurship Chapter 1.
modules.assignment: Identify three real-world problems in your community or daily life. For each problem, describe (1) who has the problem, (2) how they currently solve it (if at all), and (3) a potential business opportunity to solve it better. Then select your best idea and explain why it has entrepreneurial potential using at least two opportunity evaluation criteria.

modules.title: Business Model Canvas
modules.objectives: Identify and explain the nine blocks of the Business Model Canvas Apply the Business Model Canvas to analyze an existing business Use the Canvas to design a business model for a new venture
modules.readingNote: OpenStax Entrepreneurship — Chapter 7: Building a Business Model.
modules.videoNote: Business Model Canvas explained: all nine building blocks with examples.
modules.video2Note: How to fill out a Business Model Canvas — step-by-step tutorial.
modules.practiceNote: Complete the review questions from OpenStax Entrepreneurship Chapter 7.
modules.assignment: Complete a Business Model Canvas for your business idea from Module 7. Fill in all nine blocks (Customer Segments, Value Propositions, Channels, Customer Relationships, Revenue Streams, Key Resources, Key Activities, Key Partnerships, Cost Structure). Explain your most important trade-offs and assumptions.

quizQuestions.question: Which of the four management functions involves setting organizational goals and determining how to achieve them?
quizQuestions.answer: Planning
quizQuestions.explanation: Planning is the management function of setting objectives and deciding how best to achieve them — it is the foundation of the POLC framework.

quizQuestions.question: The 'Controlling' function of management primarily involves:
options: Monitoring performance and taking corrective action when necessary
quizQuestions.answer: Monitoring performance and taking corrective action when necessary
quizQuestions.explanation: Controlling involves measuring actual performance against planned performance and making adjustments to keep the organization on track.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "Which of the four management functions involves setting organizational goals and determining how to achieve them" is: Planning.
quizQuestions.answer: True
quizQuestions.explanation: True. Planning is correct.

quizQuestions.question: Fill in the blank: Complete: In the context of the topic, ___ refers to "which level of management is primarily responsible for day-to-day supervision of frontline workers."
quizQuestions.answer: First-line (supervisory) management
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: First-line (supervisory) management.

quizQuestions.question: A transformational leader is best characterized by:
options: Focusing on rules, rewards, and punishments to manage performance
Inspiring followers to exceed expectations through vision and purpose
Avoiding all decision-making and leaving employees alone
Strictly following established procedures without deviation
quizQuestions.answer: Inspiring followers to exceed expectations through vision and purpose
quizQuestions.explanation: Transformational leaders motivate followers by appealing to higher ideals and creating a compelling vision for change and improvement.

quizQuestions.question: An autocratic leader makes decisions:
options: By delegating all decisions to team members
By consulting the team and then reaching consensus
Independently and without input from others
By following a democratic voting process
quizQuestions.answer: Independently and without input from others
quizQuestions.explanation: Autocratic leaders centralize decision-making authority, making choices without seeking team input. This can be effective in crises but may reduce employee morale.

quizQuestions.question: Situational leadership theory suggests that effective leaders should:
options: Always use the same leadership style regardless of the situation
Adapt their leadership style based on the development level of the follower
Focus exclusively on building personal relationships with employees
Rely on rules and authority rather than personal influence
quizQuestions.answer: Adapt their leadership style based on the development level of the follower
quizQuestions.explanation: Situational leadership (Hersey and Blanchard) holds that the most effective style depends on the employee's skill and motivation level in a given task.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "A transformational leader is best characterized by:" is: Inspiring followers to exceed expectations through vision and purpose.
quizQuestions.answer: True
quizQuestions.explanation: True. Inspiring followers to exceed expectations through vision and purpose is correct.

quizQuestions.question: A company divided into departments based on the type of function performed (e.g., Marketing, Finance, HR) uses which type of organizational structure?
quizQuestions.answer: Functional structure
quizQuestions.explanation: A functional structure groups employees by specialty or function. It promotes expertise and efficiency but can create silos between departments.

quizQuestions.question: A wide span of control means a manager:
options: Directly supervises a small number of employees
Has many levels of hierarchy between them and frontline workers
Directly supervises a large number of employees
quizQuestions.answer: Directly supervises a large number of employees
quizQuestions.explanation: Span of control refers to the number of employees a manager directly supervises. A wide span means many direct reports, often found in flat organizations.

quizQuestions.question: A matrix organizational structure is characterized by:
options: Employees reporting to a single functional manager only
Employees reporting to both a functional manager and a project manager simultaneously
A flat structure with no formal managers
quizQuestions.answer: Employees reporting to both a functional manager and a project manager simultaneously
quizQuestions.explanation: Matrix structures combine functional and project-based reporting lines, enabling flexibility and cross-functional collaboration, though they can create role confusion.

quizQuestions.question: Fill in the blank: Complete: In the context of the topic, ___ refers to "a startup with few management layers and wide spans of control has a:."
quizQuestions.answer: Flat organizational structure
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Flat organizational structure.

quizQuestions.question: According to Maslow's Hierarchy of Needs, which level of needs must be met first before higher-level needs motivate behavior?
quizQuestions.answer: Physiological needs
quizQuestions.explanation: Maslow's hierarchy begins with physiological needs (food, water, shelter) as the foundation. Lower-level needs must generally be satisfied before higher-level needs become motivators.

quizQuestions.question: In Herzberg's Two-Factor Theory, 'hygiene factors' are best described as:
options: Factors that actively motivate employees to work harder
Basic job conditions whose absence causes dissatisfaction but whose presence does not motivate
Factors related to personal achievement and recognition
Intrinsic rewards that increase employee engagement
quizQuestions.answer: Basic job conditions whose absence causes dissatisfaction but whose presence does not motivate
quizQuestions.explanation: Hygiene factors (salary, working conditions, job security) prevent dissatisfaction when adequate but do not actively create motivation. Only motivators (achievement, recognition, growth) do.

quizQuestions.question: The recruitment process typically begins with:
options: Posting job listings and attracting candidate applications
Training new employees on company systems
Designing the compensation and benefits package
quizQuestions.answer: Posting job listings and attracting candidate applications
quizQuestions.explanation: Recruitment starts with identifying a staffing need, creating a job description, and attracting qualified candidates through job postings, networking, or recruiters.

quizQuestions.question: An employee who values recognition, promotion opportunities, and creative challenges is primarily motivated by which level of Maslow's hierarchy?
quizQuestions.answer: Esteem and self-actualization
quizQuestions.explanation: Recognition and achievement address esteem needs; creative challenge and personal growth relate to self-actualization — the top two levels of Maslow's hierarchy.

quizQuestions.question: Just-in-time (JIT) inventory management aims to:
options: Maintain the largest possible inventory to avoid stockouts
Receive goods only when they are needed in the production process to minimize inventory costs
quizQuestions.answer: Receive goods only when they are needed in the production process to minimize inventory costs
quizQuestions.explanation: JIT minimizes inventory holding costs by coordinating deliveries to arrive exactly when needed in production, reducing waste and storage costs.

quizQuestions.question: In supply chain management, what does 'upstream' refer to?
options: Activities closest to the end customer
The delivery and after-sales service stages
Suppliers and raw material providers feeding into the production process
quizQuestions.answer: Suppliers and raw material providers feeding into the production process
quizQuestions.explanation: Upstream activities are those early in the supply chain — raw material extraction, suppliers, and components — before production begins.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "Just-in-time (JIT) inventory management aims to:" is: Receive goods only when they are needed in the production process to minimize inventory costs.
quizQuestions.answer: True
quizQuestions.explanation: True. Receive goods only when they are needed in the production process to minimize inventory costs is correct.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "In supply chain management, what does 'upstream' refer to" is: Activities closest to the end customer.
quizQuestions.answer: False
quizQuestions.explanation: False. The correct answer is: Suppliers and raw material providers feeding into the production process.

quizQuestions.question: A SWOT analysis examines which four dimensions of a business situation?
quizQuestions.answer: Strengths, Weaknesses, Opportunities, Threats
quizQuestions.explanation: SWOT analysis examines internal factors (Strengths and Weaknesses) and external factors (Opportunities and Threats) to inform strategic planning.

quizQuestions.question: Which section of a business plan describes what the company does, who it serves, and why it will succeed — in a brief overview?
quizQuestions.answer: Executive summary
quizQuestions.explanation: The executive summary provides a concise overview of the entire business plan, covering the business concept, market opportunity, competitive advantage, and financial highlights.

quizQuestions.question: Primary market research involves:
options: Reviewing existing published reports and studies about the industry
Collecting new, firsthand data directly from potential customers through surveys or interviews
quizQuestions.answer: Collecting new, firsthand data directly from potential customers through surveys or interviews
quizQuestions.explanation: Primary research gathers original data collected specifically for the current study, providing direct insights about your target market's needs and preferences.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "A SWOT analysis examines which four dimensions of a business situation" is: Strengths, Weaknesses, Opportunities, Threats.
quizQuestions.answer: True
quizQuestions.explanation: True. Strengths, Weaknesses, Opportunities, Threats is correct.

quizQuestions.question: Which trait is most commonly associated with successful entrepreneurs?
options: Having unlimited access to capital before starting
Tolerance for ambiguity and comfort with calculated risk-taking
Preferring large established organizations over new ventures
quizQuestions.answer: Tolerance for ambiguity and comfort with calculated risk-taking
quizQuestions.explanation: Entrepreneurship inherently involves uncertainty. Successful entrepreneurs are comfortable making decisions with incomplete information and taking calculated risks.

quizQuestions.question: The difference between invention and innovation in entrepreneurship is:
options: Invention creates a new product; innovation applies ideas to create value in the marketplace
Innovation creates a new product; invention applies it commercially
Innovation is only relevant to technology companies
quizQuestions.answer: Invention creates a new product; innovation applies ideas to create value in the marketplace
quizQuestions.explanation: Invention is the creation of something new; innovation is the practical application of creative ideas to generate value — often adapting existing concepts for new markets.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "Which trait is most commonly associated with successful entrepreneurs" is: Tolerance for ambiguity and comfort with calculated risk-taking.
quizQuestions.answer: True
quizQuestions.explanation: True. Tolerance for ambiguity and comfort with calculated risk-taking is correct.

quizQuestions.question: In the Business Model Canvas, the 'Value Proposition' block describes:
options: How much revenue the business expects to generate
The bundle of products/services that creates value for a specific customer segment
The channels through which the business delivers products
The cost structure of the business
quizQuestions.answer: The bundle of products/services that creates value for a specific customer segment
quizQuestions.explanation: The value proposition is the core of the Business Model Canvas — it describes the unique value a business creates and why customers choose it over alternatives.

quizQuestions.question: The 'Customer Segments' block of the Business Model Canvas identifies:
options: The number of customers the business hopes to acquire
The specific groups of people or organizations the business aims to serve
The geographic regions where the business operates
The marketing budget allocated to different customer groups
quizQuestions.answer: The specific groups of people or organizations the business aims to serve
quizQuestions.explanation: Customer Segments define the different groups of people or entities the business targets. Understanding segments enables tailored value propositions and marketing strategies.

quizQuestions.question: The 'Revenue Streams' block of the Business Model Canvas describes:
options: How the business generates cash from each customer segment
The total market size for the business
quizQuestions.answer: How the business generates cash from each customer segment
quizQuestions.explanation: Revenue Streams represent the ways in which a company generates income from its customer segments — through sales, subscriptions, licensing, advertising, or other models.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "In the Business Model Canvas, the 'Value Proposition' block describes:" is: The bundle of products/services that creates value for a specific customer segment.
quizQuestions.answer: True
quizQuestions.explanation: True. The bundle of products/services that creates value for a specific customer segment is correct.

questions.question: Which management function involves determining the best course of action to achieve organizational goals before any work begins?
questions.explanation: Planning is the first POLC function — it sets the objectives and strategy before resources are organized or tasks are performed.

questions.question: A manager who uses positive feedback, bonuses, and performance reviews to drive employee behavior is primarily using which leadership approach?
questions.explanation: Transactional leadership relies on reward-and-punishment exchanges to manage performance — clear expectations with bonuses for achievement and consequences for failure.

questions.question: An organization with many management layers between the CEO and frontline workers is described as:
questions.explanation: Tall organizations have many hierarchical levels, narrow spans of control, and clear chains of command — common in large corporations and bureaucracies.

questions.question: True or False: The answer to "According to Herzberg's Two-Factor Theory, which of the following is a 'motivator' rather than a 'hygiene factor'" is: Recognition for achievement.
questions.explanation: True. Recognition for achievement is correct.

questions.question: Just-in-time (JIT) inventory management was pioneered by which company and aims to reduce:
questions.explanation: JIT originated in Toyota's production system (TPS). It minimizes inventory holding costs by receiving materials exactly when needed, reducing waste.

questions.question: The SWOT analysis tool helps a business assess:
options: Both internal strengths/weaknesses and external opportunities/threats
questions.answer: Both internal strengths/weaknesses and external opportunities/threats
questions.explanation: SWOT is a strategic planning framework that systematically examines both internal factors (Strengths, Weaknesses) and external factors (Opportunities, Threats).

questions.question: An entrepreneur who spots an opportunity in the ridesharing market and creates a new app to address it is demonstrating:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Opportunity recognition.

questions.question: True or False: The answer to "Which block of the Business Model Canvas describes the most important things a company must do to make its business model work" is: Key Resources.
questions.explanation: False. The correct answer is: Key Activities.

questions.question: A startup that gives up 20% equity in exchange for $400,000 in venture capital funding has an implied company valuation (pre-money) of:
questions.explanation: If 20% equity = $400,000, then 100% = $2,000,000. Pre-money valuation is the company's value before the investment.

questions.question: When a startup conducts a focus group with 10 potential customers to refine their product concept, they are conducting:
questions.explanation: Primary research involves collecting new data directly from the source (potential customers in a focus group) rather than using existing published data.

questions.question: A business model that charges customers a recurring fee (monthly or annually) for continued access to a product or service is called:
questions.explanation: Subscription models generate predictable, recurring revenue by charging customers at regular intervals for ongoing access to a product or service (e.g., Netflix, Spotify).

questions.question: True or False: The answer to "The unity of command principle in management states that:" is: Each employee should report to only one supervisor to avoid conflicting instructions.
questions.explanation: True. Each employee should report to only one supervisor to avoid conflicting instructions is correct.

questions.question: Which management level is primarily responsible for translating top management's strategy into actionable plans for frontline teams?
questions.explanation: Middle managers bridge the gap between strategic direction (from executives) and operational execution (by frontline workers), translating strategy into department-level plans.

questions.question: A lean startup approach encourages entrepreneurs to:
options: Spend as much time as possible perfecting the product before launching
Build a minimum viable product (MVP), test it with customers, and iterate based on feedback
Avoid all customer contact until the product is fully developed
questions.answer: Build a minimum viable product (MVP), test it with customers, and iterate based on feedback
questions.explanation: The Lean Startup methodology (Eric Ries) emphasizes rapid experimentation through an MVP — build, measure, learn — to validate assumptions with minimal waste.

questions.question: Operations management adds value to an organization by:
options: Transforming inputs (labor, materials, capital) into finished goods and services efficiently
questions.answer: Transforming inputs (labor, materials, capital) into finished goods and services efficiently
questions.explanation: Operations management oversees the process of converting resources into products or services, focusing on efficiency, quality, and continuous improvement.

questions.question: True or False: The answer to "A CEO sets a 5-year vision for the company; a plant manager schedules daily production. This illustrates the difference between:" is: Organizing and controlling.
questions.explanation: False. The correct answer is: Strategic and operational planning.

questions.question: Emotional intelligence (EQ) in leadership refers to:
options: The ability to solve complex mathematical problems
The capacity to recognize, understand, and manage one's own emotions and those of others
A leader's technical expertise in the company's industry
The number of employees a leader can effectively supervise
questions.answer: The capacity to recognize, understand, and manage one's own emotions and those of others
questions.explanation: EQ encompasses self-awareness, self-regulation, empathy, and social skills — traits increasingly recognized as critical to effective leadership.

questions.question: Decentralization in an organization means that:
options: All decisions are made by top management
Decision-making authority is distributed to lower-level managers and employees
The company has multiple office locations
The organization eliminates all management positions
questions.answer: Decision-making authority is distributed to lower-level managers and employees
questions.explanation: Decentralization pushes decision-making downward, enabling faster responses and greater employee autonomy, though it requires strong communication systems.

questions.question: McClelland's Theory of Needs identifies three core motivators. An employee who is most driven by influencing others and leading teams has a high need for:
questions.explanation: McClelland's three needs are Achievement (drive to excel), Affiliation (desire for relationships), and Power (desire to influence and lead). A high power need drives leadership ambition.

questions.question: True or False: The answer to "A company that disrupts an existing industry by offering a radically simpler, more affordable product to a new customer segment is using which strategy" is: Disruptive innovation.
questions.explanation: True. Disruptive innovation is correct.

questions.question: The Business Model Canvas block that describes how a company communicates and delivers its value proposition to customers is:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Channels.

questions.question: A general partnership differs from a limited partnership in that in a general partnership:
options: All partners have limited liability protection
All partners share unlimited personal liability for the business's debts
One partner manages while others are passive investors
Partners must incorporate at the state level
questions.answer: All partners share unlimited personal liability for the business's debts
questions.explanation: In a general partnership, all partners are personally liable for business debts. In a limited partnership, limited partners have liability only up to their investment.

questions.question: A startup's 'runway' refers to:
options: How long the startup can operate before running out of cash at its current burn rate
The startup's plan for reaching profitability
The physical space used for product testing
questions.answer: How long the startup can operate before running out of cash at its current burn rate
questions.explanation: Runway = Cash on Hand / Monthly Burn Rate. It tells founders and investors how many months the company can operate before needing additional funding.

questions.question: True or False: The answer to "A company's total addressable market (TAM) is $5 billion. If they capture 2% market share in year one, their projected year-one revenue is:" is: $2 million.
questions.explanation: False. The correct answer is: $100 million.

questions.question: Supply chain resilience refers to a supply chain's ability to:
options: Minimize all costs at every stage regardless of risk
Withstand and recover from disruptions such as natural disasters, supplier failures, or geopolitical events
Eliminate all international suppliers and source domestically only
Process the maximum volume of orders per hour
questions.answer: Withstand and recover from disruptions such as natural disasters, supplier failures, or geopolitical events
questions.explanation: Supply chain resilience is the capacity to prepare for, respond to, and recover from disruptions while maintaining continuous business operations.

questions.question: A startup that first sells to innovators and early adopters before reaching the mainstream market is navigating the:
questions.explanation: Everett Rogers' diffusion of innovation curve shows how products spread from innovators → early adopters → early majority → late majority → laggards.

questions.question: Greenwashing damages a business in the long run primarily because:
options: It results in criminal prosecution in all cases
It erodes customer trust and brand credibility when the deception is discovered
It prevents the company from entering new markets
questions.answer: It erodes customer trust and brand credibility when the deception is discovered
questions.explanation: When greenwashing is exposed, it creates reputational damage, customer backlash, and potential regulatory penalties — undermining long-term brand equity.

questions.question: True or False: The answer to "In an effective investor pitch, the 'ask' slide should clearly state:" is: How much funding is being requested, what it will be used for, and what investors will receive in return.
questions.explanation: True. How much funding is being requested, what it will be used for, and what investors will receive in return is correct.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a 'moat' (competitive advantage) that makes a startup difficult to compete with?
options: A patented technology that competitors cannot legally copy
Having more social media followers than competitors
Charging the same price as all competitors
questions.answer: A patented technology that competitors cannot legally copy
questions.explanation: A moat is a durable competitive advantage — a patented technology creates a legal barrier that prevents competitors from replicating the product, protecting market position.

questions.question: When a company enters a new market with an existing product, this growth strategy is called:
questions.explanation: The Ansoff Matrix identifies market development as selling existing products to new markets (new geographic regions, customer segments, or distribution channels).

questions.question: A triple bottom line (TBL) business measures success using which three dimensions?
questions.explanation: The Triple Bottom Line framework evaluates business success across three dimensions: social responsibility (people), environmental impact (planet), and financial performance (profit).

questions.question: True or False: The answer to "A minimum viable product (MVP) is designed to:" is: Be the most feature-rich version of the product possible.
questions.explanation: False. The correct answer is: Test core assumptions with the least amount of development effort and cost.

questions.question: Which type of business growth strategy involves acquiring another company in a related but different industry to gain new capabilities or markets?
questions.explanation: Related diversification involves expanding into adjacent industries or product lines that share some connection with the current business, creating potential synergies.

questions.question: An entrepreneur who values making a social impact as much as profit would most likely pursue:
options: A publicly traded C-Corporation focused on shareholder returns
A B Corporation or social enterprise business model
questions.answer: A B Corporation or social enterprise business model
questions.explanation: B Corporations and social enterprises are designed for entrepreneurs who want to create measurable social and environmental impact alongside financial sustainability.

questions.question: When delivering a business pitch, addressing a potential investor's objection proactively (before they raise it) is a technique called:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Inoculation.
